'''Bloodletter''' was the nickname of a corporate [[Spy/Legends|spy]] hired in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]] by the [[Vratix/Legends|Vratix]] [[Ashern]] group to infiltrate the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]].<ref name="BactaBasics1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031106thyferra1|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 1: The Thriving Season}}</ref> Surviving a close encounter with a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] in blue and silver [[Armor/Legends|armor]],<ref name="BactaBasics3">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120thyferra3|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 3: Fields of Dreams}}</ref> Bloodletter discovered the existence of the hybrid [[kolcta]].<ref name="BactaBasics4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031126thyferra4|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 4: The Kolcta Generation}}</ref> Soon after the [[Bacta War]], Bloodletter released a memoir of the [[Alazhixazha|Thriving Season]], ''[[Thrive or Die]]''. Though an infamous figure, the Bloodletter's identity and even gender remains unknown.<ref name="BactaBasics1" />
